title: Startup founder interview
explanation:
Startup founder interview

Listen to an interview with a tech founder.

Transcript

Interviewer: What almost killed the company in year two?
Founder: Cash flow. We scaled too fast and underestimated how long enterprise clients take to sign contracts. We had to cut staff and renegotiate with investors. It was painful, but it forced us to focus on sustainable growth rather than hype.

questions.text: The main problem in year two was [*cash flow|product design|office location].
They scaled [*too fast|too slowly|not at all].
Enterprise clients take a long time to [*sign contracts|visit offices|choose logos].
The crisis forced a focus on [*sustainable growth|publicity stunts|luxury offices].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: They had to cut ____.  | *staff | coffee | windows
They previously chased too much ____.  | *hype | sleep | silence
The lesson concerns [*business discipline|recipe design|sports training].
The company survived by [*changing strategy|ignoring investors|stopping sales].
